name: credits-ledger
description: Application-controlled internal credits ledger with issuance, transfers, burns, and indexed activity views.
use_case: Use Xian as the authoritative ledger for app credits, balances, grants, burns, and transfer history while normal Python services project and serve app-facing reads.

starter_flows.name: local
starter_flows.summary: Run the pack locally with indexed reads, dashboard access, monitoring, and the deeper reference-app API/projector flow.

steps.title: Create and start the local indexed node
commands: uv run xian network create credits-ledger-local --template single-node-indexed --chain-id credits-ledger-local-1 --generate-validator-key --init-node
uv run xian node start validator-1
uv run xian node endpoints validator-1
notes: Run these commands from the xian-cli checkout.
Use the reported RPC, dashboard, and monitoring URLs as the authoritative local endpoints.

steps.title: Bootstrap the credits ledger contract
notes: Run these commands from the xian-py checkout.
Set XIAN_NODE_URL, XIAN_CHAIN_ID, and XIAN_WALLET_PRIVATE_KEY for the bootstrap wallet.

steps.title: Run the projected read model and API
notes: The projector backfills indexed events first, then tails new ledger activity into SQLite.
The API exposes both authoritative chain reads and projected balance/activity views.

starter_flows.name: remote
starter_flows.summary: Deploy the pack on a Linux host with xian-deploy while keeping the same contract bootstrap and projection/API pattern.

steps.title: Create the remote-oriented network bundle
commands: uv run xian network create credits-ledger-remote --template embedded-backend --chain-id credits-ledger-remote-1 --generate-validator-key
notes: Run this command from the xian-cli checkout.
Use the generated network/profile material as the local source of truth before deploying remote hosts.

steps.title: Deploy and verify the remote node
notes: Run these commands from the xian-deploy checkout with your prepared inventory and group vars.
Use the remote health playbook as the first full validation of BDS, monitoring, and runtime state.

steps.title: Bootstrap the contract and run the application side
notes: Run these commands from the xian-py checkout against the remote RPC/BDS endpoints.
Use the same projector/API pattern as the local flow, just pointed at the deployed node.
